🚨BREAKING -- Four Palestine Action activists who raided an Elbit Systems factory in August 2024 have been found guilty of criminal damage.

Samuel Corner, Charlotte Head, Leona Kamio and Fatema Zainab Rajwani were found guilty of criminal damage today, while Corner was also found guilty of GBH without intent.

The two other defendants, Zoe Rogers and Jordan Devlin, were found not guilty.

kept.text:
PALESTINE ACTION "ACTIVISTS" CONVICTED

Four Palestine Action "activists" Charlotte Head, Samuel Corner, Leona Kamio and Fatema Rajwani were convicted after forcing entry into a UK site run by Elbit Systems and causing extensive damage.

Using a van to break through security, they entered the facility and deliberately smashed equipment, drones and infrastructure. Corner was also found guilty of grievous bodily harm after striking a police sergeant with a sledgehammer.

Zoe Rogers and Jordan Devlin were acquitted, but the outcome is clear this was organised destruction, not a "protest."

profile_bio.description: Jurors have a right to acquit a defendant according to their conscience, irrespective of a Judge’s directions. Every defendant deserves access to fair justice.

kept.text:
Four Palestine Action activists have been found guilty of criminal damage at a site owned by Israeli weapons firm Elbit Systems – while two others have been acquitted of the same charge. 

Charlotte Head, Samuel Corner, Leona Kamio and Fatema Rajwani – all aged between 21 and 30 – took part in a raid at the facility near Bristol in August 2024. Palestine Action's stated goal is to disrupt the supply of weapons used in the genocide in Gaza.

Another two activists, Zoe Rogers, 22, and Jordan Devlin, 31, were cleared of criminal damage charges by a jury at Woolwich Crown Court on Tuesday.

Corner was also found guilty of inflicting grievous bodily harm (GBH) without intent. Prosecutors had accused him of GBH with intent after he hit a police officer with a sledgehammer during the raid, but the jury cleared him of that more serious charge. 

The convicted activists smashed into a security perimeter at the site with a van before destroying property inside with tools, the court previously heard.

"Inside the warehouse, they set about destroying as much property as they could,” prosecutor Deanna Heer KC told the court.

"They used crowbars and hammers to damage computers, equipment, drones and other products Elbit had manufactured," she said. "Their role was to cause as much damage as possible until they were forcibly stopped."

During a closing speech delivered late last month, Rogers told the court: “I am so proud that I took part in this. I am proud that I overcame my fear and took action. Because of course I was scared. You don't just break into an Israeli weapons factory for fun."

She added:  "I can say with absolute certainty that this is the best thing I have ever done, because there is a good chance that because of our actions that night, innocent lives were saved.”

Head, Corner, Kamio and Rajwani have been remanded to prison ahead of their sentencing. All defendants have already served 18 months in prison, apart from Corner who has served 21 months.

kept.text:
A UK court has convicted four Palestine Action activists of criminal damage after a break-in at an Israeli-owned Elbit Systems arms factory near Bristol in August 2024.

Leona Kamio, Samuel Corner, Fatema Rajwani, and Charlotte Head were found guilty at Woolwich Crown Court after more than 14 hours of jury deliberations.

 Corner was acquitted of grievous bodily harm with intent but convicted of a lesser assault charge, while two other defendants were acquitted of criminal damage.

The case stemmed from damage to equipment and military-related technology during the raid, which the defendants said they believed was justified.
